data.name: AmbiguousConcatenation_Message
data.value: Both operands are `String` values; using the `&` string concatenation operator here would be more explicit about the intent here.

data.name: BitwiseOperator_Message
data.value: The result of this operation is resolved using bitwise arithmetic. This could produce unexpected results if the expectation is conventional logic here. If this isn't intentional, it could be a bug.

data.name: EmptyCodeBlock_Message
data.value: Empty code block; implement a body or consider removing it.

data.name: EmptyIfBlock_Message
data.value: Empty If block; consider reversing the conditional expression.

data.name: EnumerationOverArray_Message
data.value: Array enumeration is inefficient and would perform better with a `For...Next` construct instead, with indexed (subscript) item access.

data.name: ImplicitDateSerialConversion_Message
data.value: Implicit `DateSerial` conversion; using `VBA.DateTime` module functions to perform date and time operations would make the intent clearer while improving readability by raising the abstraction level a bit.

data.name: ImplicitLetCoercion_message
data.value: Implicit `Let` coercion (`{toType.Name}`) from `{fromType.Name}`; invoking the object's default member explicitly avoids "magic" procedure calls that are hard to follow and to fully understand without tracing obscure hidden attributes.

data.name: ImplicitNarrowingConversion_Message
data.value: Implicit narrowing conversion from a numeric type to another with a smaller range - watch out, arithmetic overflow is technically possible here.

data.name: ImplicitNumericCoercion_Message
data.value: Implicit numeric coercion (`{toType.Name}`) from `{fromType.Name}`; using an explicit type conversion function would improve the traceability of the involved data types.

data.name: ImplicitStringCoercion_Message
data.value: Implicit `String` coercion from `{fromType.Name}`; consider using an explicit type conversion (for example `CStr`) to make this operation more intentional.

data.name: ImplicitWideningConversion_Message
data.value: Implicit widening conversion from a numeric type to another with a wider range; using an explicit type conversion function here would make this operation more intentional.

data.name: LateBoundMemberAccess_Message
data.value: Late bound member access; VBA will not resolve this call until run-time, which can lead to errors that are avoidable by using a declared type more specific than `Variant` or `Object` whenever possible.

data.name: PreferErrRaiseOverErrorStatement_Message
data.value: Use the `Err.Raise` method instead of the legacy `Error` statement to raise run-time errors; like several others, this statement exists for backward compatibility with previous iterations of the BASIC programming language.

data.name: SllFailure_Message
data.value: SLL parser prediction mode failed here; if possible, rephrasing this instruction could improve language server performance.

data.name: SuspiciousValueAssignment_Message
data.value: Suspicious value assignment; since both LHS and RHS are object types, it looks like a reference assignment may have been intended. Could it be missing a `Set` keyword?

data.name: TypeCastConversion_Message
data.value: Assignment is converting the RHS object to a compatible interface type.

data.name: UnintendedConstantExpression_Message
data.value: Possibly unintended constant expression; this operation does not affect the value.

data.name: UnresolvedLateBoundMemberAccess_Message
data.value: Unresolved late bound member access; this call is likely going to raise run-time error 438 (VBR00438) at run-time.
